stance: BULLISH — 8 strict claims · 6 beats + 2 hits · GM expansion through tariff drag
summary: Apple delivered 6 strict BEATs and 2 HITs across 8 independent forward claims (revenue growth + gross margin guidance) — weighted MCS 0.875. Most recent 4 quarters all hit the HIGH end of GM guidance despite $900M-$1.4B tariff drag per quarter. Q1 FY26 was AAPL's best-ever quarter ($143.76B rev, +15.7% YoY) and Q2 FY26 set a March-quarter record at $111.18B. TTM revenue $451.4B (+12.8% YoY); operating margin expanded to 32.3% in Q2 FY26. Q3 FY26 guidance: +14-17% revenue growth, OpEx $18.8-19.1B. Stock at $293.32 (May 8 close) near 52w high $294.76, up +49% over 1 year. Sell-side target $309.75 (+5.6% upside). Tariff/iPhone supply remain headwinds; AI strategic execution is the wild card.
quotes: Today, Apple is proud to report $111.2 billion in revenue, up 17% from a year ago and a March quarter record, which was above the high end of our guidance range despite supply constraints.
Company gross margin was 49.3%, above the high end of our guidance range and up 110 basis points sequentially.
We expect our June quarter total company revenue to grow by 14% to 17% year-over-year, which comprehends our best view of constraint supply.
We expect our December quarter total company revenue to grow by 10% to 12% year-over-year, which will be our best quarter ever.
We expect gross margin to be between 46% and 47%, which includes the estimated impact of the $1.1 billion tariff-related costs that Tim referred to earlier.
We are reporting our best-ever quarter with $143.8 billion in revenue, up 16% from a year ago and exceeding our expectations.
bull: Strong MCS — 6 BEATs + 2 HITs across 8 strict claims
Weighted MCS 0.875. Six of eight guidance forecasts (revenue growth + gross margin) beat the HIGH end of the guided range. Average GM beat magnitude is +0.6-1.3pp above the high end.
Q1 FY26 ($143.8B) and Q2 FY26 ($111.2B) were record quarters. Q3 FY26 guide of +14-17% YoY would produce another record June-quarter at ~$98-100B (vs $94.04B prior).
GM held steady or expanded despite $900M-$1.4B/quarter tariff costs. Q2 FY26 GM 49.27% is highest in the series. Tariff drag absorbed via mix shift to Services and operational leverage.
Services revenue ~$26-27B/quarter growing ~14% YoY — highest-margin segment. App Store + Apple Pay + iCloud + Music + TV+ + ads — sticky and accretive to GM.
Generational hardware refresh tied to Apple Intelligence (Apple's AI). iPhone 17/Pro family expected Sep 2026 with deeper AI features driving upgrade cycle.
Returning capital aggressively at ~5% of mkt cap annualized. Share count -1-2% YoY supporting EPS growth even without revenue growth.
bear: P/E FY1 33.61× = +44% premium vs Named Competitors median 23.35×. EV/EBITDA FY1 25.69× = +55% premium vs peer median 16.55×. Multiple compression risk if growth slows.
Trump administration tariff policy creates ongoing uncertainty. $1.4B/quarter run rate could escalate if trade tensions intensify (China/India/Vietnam exposure).
Q2 FY26 specifically flagged constrained iPhone supply impacting growth. Q3 FY26 guidance comprehends best view of constraint supply — actual delivery risk.
Apple Intelligence rollout slower than MSFT Copilot / Google Gemini / OpenAI products. Risk of installed-base erosion if AI-native consumer features underwhelm.
EU Digital Markets Act restrictions on App Store; US DOJ ruling against Google search remedies could eliminate ~$20B/year Apple search-default payment.
Services growth running ~14% YoY but base getting larger; deceleration to single-digits would compress overall growth and multiple.
trigger_up: Q3 FY26 ER (Jul 30, 2026) revenue above $100B (+15-17%) with GM 48%+
Apple Intelligence iPhone 17 launch (Sep 2026) drives upgrade cycle acceleration
China tariff resolution / reduction below $500M/quarter run rate
Multiple expansion to 35-37× FY1 P/E on accelerating growth
trigger_down: Q3 FY26 ER misses revenue guide (sub $97B) on iPhone supply constraints
Apple Intelligence iPhone 17 reception underwhelms vs Galaxy AI / Pixel
DOJ Google ruling eliminates Apple's search-default payment
bottom_line: Apple is delivering an exceptional execution cycle — 6 strict BEATs + 2 HITs across 8 independent forward claims, two consecutive record quarters (Q1 FY26 $143.8B, Q2 FY26 $111.2B), gross margin expanding to 49.3% despite tariff drag, and Q3 FY26 guide of +14-17% YoY. Stock at $293.32 sits just below the 52w high ($294.76) with 49% trailing-year gain. Sell-side target $309.75 implies modest +5.6% upside, reflecting valuation discipline. P/E FY1 33.6× is +44% premium vs peer median but defensible given execution quality. Key catalysts: Apple Intelligence rollout, iPhone 17 launch (Sep 2026), tariff resolution. Stance: BULLISH on execution; valuation premium requires continued operational excellence.
disclaimer: Quoted statements verbatim from AAPL earnings calls in /AAPL/Transcripts/ directory. Fundamentals from EDGAR XBRL companyfacts (CIK 0000320193). FactSet peer data captured 2026-05-12. AAPL fiscal year ends late September. MCS uses strict scoring (BEAT = above HIGH end, HIT = within range, MISS = below LOW end). Not investment advice.
